text:
It was autumn when I found it. I found this gem nestled among the gnarled bark of an ancient oak—I call it a gem because it was like nothing else I had ever seen.

That weekend, I decided to spend hours wandering these woods. My feet found their way to that particular oak at dusk. I climbed up the gnarled trunk, feeling its resistance. Once I reached its treetop nest, there stood the item—I can’t explain it, but it had an allure. Maybe it was the way the light danced around it, or how its surface seemed to breathe fireflies.

Carefully, I placed my hand upon it. It felt warmer than my other hand. I brought it down to reveal…just a normal-looking book? No. There were shapes. Patterns. I traced one with my finger…it was a map. I looked closer; each direction was denoted by different letters. Some pointed upwards, some downwards.
